resources.string: Not require Root permissions, only the download module function is available.

Patching Android kernel and system. A new kernel-based root solution with support for modules similar to Magisk and kernel function injection (requires inline-hook and syscall-table-hook). Derived and modified from KernelSU.
An advanced kernel-based root solution with Magic Mount support, enhanced control over userspace apps, and customizable root privileges.
A kernel-based root solution offering enhanced control over userspace apps, customizable root privileges, and systemless module support using OverlayFS.
A suite of tools for Android customization, offering root access, module support, boot image modification, and process-level code execution with Zygisk.

A permission-based solution that allows apps to execute system-level commands without full root access. It requires authorization from Sui or Shizuku, enabling elevated privileges for specific operations while maintaining system integrity.
Are you sure that you want reboot your device?
Do you want to install or download this version of the module?
Seems like that you device doesn\'t support this module version. Install at your own risk!
Runs code later during boot (more system initialized).
Runs code early after boot (partially booted system).
Sets system properties loaded by Magisk\'s resetprop.
Allows modules to interact with the Zygisk framework, enabling system-level modifications and interactions.
Including additional APKs inside the module.
Web interface for managing modules. The user has to confirm the usage of various JavaScript APIs.
Allows the user executing a script via a button click
Show some love to this module developer by donating the developer a coffee! ☕
Are you sure that you want to install [color=surface_tint]%1$s[/color]?
Are you sure that you want to install [color=surface_tint]%1$s[/color]? This module requires also:
Seems like that you have a too old root provider version. Check if there are any updates for your root provider.
Seems like that your device doesn\'t have the support for the required architecture declared by the developer.
Seems like that your devices is not supported by this modules or has been excluded.
Documentations, Supported Repos and anything else
Change the app to your needs
Enables text wrap inside the install terminal
Run module WebUIs in a separate portable companion app
The downloaded module file could not be found. It may have been moved or deleted.
The selected module file could not be opened.
The selected file is not a readable module archive.
Change color scheme and night mode.
Delete zip of module after the installation is successful, this will be executed by Superuser
Workaround DNS poisoning in some nations
If enabled you have to confirm every reboot
Listed modules that may closed source or dangerous
Manage your preferences on the updating process
Select the start screen of the app
Automatically update repos in the background
Select the time interval for updating the repositories
Automatically checks for App Updates every time you open the app
Doesn\'t matter if it is a real or fake one
Enables update check for installed modules
Select the time interval for checking your installed modules
Clear Install Terminal when installing more than one Module
Uses shell instead of creating files in the module directory. Once enabled you won\'t able to use the restore button due limitations.
Change the behavior of handlers for modules or many more about modules
To simplify debugging, provide a local domain such as http://localhost. See the alert above for more.

WebUI will now consistently use your provided address for all modules, bypass SSL errors, and disable the access to /data/adb/modules/{ID}/webroot. Note that a self-signed local web server is required to use this feature, which is intended for debugging purposes only, is not meant for everyday use, and is used at your own risk.
Provide modules repositories and Manage modules for Magisk and KernelSU.
Don\'t worry, MMRL updated your installed repos for you. 😋
There are no updates for repositories
MMRL is, and always will be, free, and open source. You can however show me that you care by making a donation.
New version [italic]%s[/italic] is available, click to upgrade.
This is not a valid local domain!
Are you sure that you wanna cancel the installation?
Are you sure that you wanna cancel this action?
Most root providers do not support an action CLI command. Instead, you can use a generic script

This module has been blacklisted by MMRL. Installation is disabled to ensure your security. If you still want to install this module, you can disable blacklist alerts in the settings by navigating to [bold]Settings → Security → Blacklist Alerts[/bold].
Display an alert for local and explore modules, and additionally block the installation of any blacklisted modules
Allow JavaScript APIs and much more about local modules
Insets require the import of the MMRLs insets feature.
Are you sure that you want to install this module?
Get the most out of MMRL!
Allow access to the Advanced KernelSU API?
Do you want to allow this module to use the Advanced KernelSU API? If you don’t trust it, you can deny access
Allow access to the FileSystem API?
Do you want to allow this module to use the FileSystem API? If you don’t trust it, you can deny access
Keep the provider running in the background to significantly reduce startup times and improve overall responsiveness

By changing the Working Mode, you are required to restart the app and all associated services for the new mode to be properly applied. Please note that by pressing \"Apply\", you\'re initiating the process that will restart the app and all services to ensure the changes take effect as expected.
Failed to close the action execution
This module includes a MMRL only WebUI.
This module uses a config file for WebUI\'s in MMRL
This module maybe used the FileSystem API provided by MMRL.
This module has a toggable developer console.
This module supports WebUI Plugins to add new JavaScript APIs.
A Module ID was not defined
Are you sure you want to exit?
Shortcuts are not supported on your device or launcher

To access all features of MMRL, a rooted Android device is required. MMRL does not provide instructions for rooting and is not responsible for any damage that may occur during the rooting process or from the use of MMRL itself.\n\n[link=https://mmrl.dev/legal/privacy]Privacy Policy[/link] • [link=https://mmrl.dev/legal/terms]Terms of Service[/link]

To enhance your experience and provide more robust WebUI functionality, MMRL now utilizes a dedicated external application: WebUI X: Portable. This allows for better performance, improved security, and more frequent updates for WebUI features. You\'ll need to install WebUI X: Portable to continue accessing WebUI\'s for your modules. Users who installed MMRL via the Google Play Store will be redirected to the Google Play Store page for WebUI X: Portable.
Shows line numbers on the left side of the terminal
The date pattern will be parsed as:\n[color=primary]%1$s[/color]
Tailor the terminal to suit your preferences
Discover and install modules from [bold]%1$d[/bold] trusted repositories, featuring over [bold]%2$d[/bold] modules for your device.
Set a custom spoofed WebUI X Portable Package name.
Failed to update App Profile for %s
Failed to update SELinux rules for %s
Create your own profile with flexible options, including rules, permissions, security context, and more.
Modify the settings of your custom profile.
Granting root access gives this app advanced control over your device, including system files and settings.
The global default value for \"Umount modules\" in App Profile. If enabled, it will remove all module modifications to the system for apps that don\'t have a profile set.
Temporarily disable the ability of any app to gain root privileges via the ⁠su command (Existing root processes won\'t be affected).
All modules are up to date
Review version changes, source, compatibility, scripts, and reboot impact before installing.
%1$d compatible updates will be downloaded and passed to the existing bulk installer.
Contains changes that should be reviewed
Downloads, module changes, failures, and technical logs
No operations have been recorded yet
This permanently removes completed operation summaries and technical logs. Running operations remain visible and are not cancelled.
Showing the latest part of this log. Copy or share to view the full output.
Repository metadata is shown here before download. The complete archive is inspected before installation and the exact findings are saved to Activity.
The repository does not declare special files. The downloaded ZIP will still be inspected.
Inspecting scripts, binaries and privileged changes
Progress and technical details are also available in Activity.
Archive checks passed. Review the warnings above before installing.
Reading cached metadata and preparing modules.
Connect to the internet to refresh this repository. Cached modules remain available when present.
Try another module name, ID, developer, or category.
This repository has not published any modules yet.
The downloaded ZIP will be verified and inspected for scripts, native binaries, APKs, SELinux policy, system properties, path traversal, and unsafe compression before installation.
Attempt to retain version %1$s before staging
Module updates ignored. Re-enable update tracking from the module details screen.
Removed an incomplete empty download and retried.
Refresh enabled repositories and check installed modules immediately
Enter a module ID or Tasker variable
Enter an operation ID or Tasker variable
Enter a module ID or direct ZIP URL
Whether MMRL accepted or completed the request
Download, update, install, action, or other operation type
Progress from 0 to 100, or -1 when unavailable
Whether the installed module is enabled
Whether a newer tracked version exists
Whether update tracking is disabled for this module
Repository URL supplying the newest version
Whether an operation is waiting for reboot
Whether Activity can safely roll back the operation
Temporary read-only content URI for the technical log
Number of returned modules or updates
Controlled module automation with approval, Activity logs, and reviewed installs
Controls queued downloads and privileged actions. Read-only status and query actions remain available.
Allow Tasker to queue module ZIP downloads through MMRL.
Allow Tasker to request module state changes.
Runs only a module\'s bundled action.sh; arbitrary shell commands are never accepted.
Allow removal requests and restoration from retained rollback archives.
Requires a short-lived token bound to the inspected archive hash.
Every privileged action waits for an MMRL approval notification.
Execute immediately only while the device is unlocked; otherwise ask.
Execute allowed module IDs immediately and ask for all others.
Read-only actions and downloads can still be used when enabled.
No modules are preapproved. Enter one module ID per line.
Short-lived token bound to the reviewed archive hash
Unix time in milliseconds when the review token expires
Whether the operation is waiting for an MMRL approval notification
Human-readable scripts, binaries, APK, policy, and risk summary
Configure AshReXcue rescue thresholds, boot signals, and queued changes
